name: Microsoft Kernel Memory
pricing_notes: Open source; pay for Azure services used (Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Search, Azure Blob)

auth.notes: Azure AD or API key; configurable per backend service (Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Search)

performance.notes: Ingestion async (background processing); query latency 300-2000ms depending on Azure AI Search tier

agent_readiness.notes: Microsoft's RAG-as-a-service built on Semantic Kernel. Handles document ingestion, chunking, embedding, and retrieval. Supports PDF, Word, images, web pages. Azure AI Search, Qdrant, Postgres pgvector backends. REST API or C#/Python SDK. Integrates naturally with Semantic Kernel agent workflows. Excellent for Azure-native stacks.

gotchas: C# primary SDK — Python SDK is secondary and less feature-complete
Azure-native: best value when already using Azure services
Async ingestion means documents not immediately queryable
Less flexible than LlamaIndex/LangChain for custom RAG pipelines
best_when: Azure-native organizations building RAG on Microsoft stack
Teams using Semantic Kernel for agent orchestration
Enterprise RAG with Azure AI Search for compliance/security
avoid_when: Teams needing highly customized chunking/retrieval strategies (use LlamaIndex)
summary: Microsoft Kernel Memory is an open-source RAG-as-a-service built on Semantic Kernel, handling document ingestion, chunking, embedding, and retrieval. Supports PDF/Word/images with Azure AI Search, Qdrant, or Postgres pgvector backends. REST API + C#/Python SDKs. Best for Azure-native Microsoft stacks; C# SDK is more complete than Python. Integrates natively with Semantic Kernel agent orchestration.
